name: Net Profit
definition: The money a webshop ultimately keeps: everything earned minus everything spent. The apex both the money-earned and money-saved branches ladder up into.

levers.action: Grow revenue faster than variable cost

name: Revenue
definition: Total money earned from orders in a period. The headline money-earned KPI.

parents.note: Revenue is the money-earned half of profit.

name: Gross Margin
definition: Share of revenue left after the variable cost of selling (goods, shipping, fees, returns). The headline money-saved KPI: lifting it is money saved.

parents.note: Higher margin per order flows straight to profit.

levers.action: Cut returns, fee and discount leakage

name: Contribution Margin per Order
definition: Money left from an average order after all variable costs (goods, shipping, fulfillment, fees). What each order actually contributes to fixed cost and profit.
formula: Average Order Value - Variable Cost per Order

parents.note: Each order's contribution scales to profit.

levers.action: Raise AOV without raising variable cost

name: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
definition: Total margin a customer is expected to generate across their whole relationship with the shop. Connects retention to long-run profit.
formula: Average Order Value x Purchase Frequency x Customer Lifespan x Gross Margin %

parents.note: Higher lifetime value per customer lifts long-run profit.

benchmarks.label: Repeat spend
benchmarks.value: Existing customers spend ~67% more than new ones

name: Orders
definition: Number of completed purchases in a period.

parents.note: One of the two factors of revenue.

name: Sessions / Traffic
definition: Visits to the shop in a period, the top of the funnel.

parents.note: Traffic is one factor of orders.

name: Conversion Rate
definition: Share of sessions that result in an order. The most-watched funnel KPI and the product of every funnel stage.
formula: Product-View Rate x Add-to-Cart Rate x Cart-to-Checkout Rate x Checkout-Completion Rate

parents.note: The other factor of orders.
More orders per click lowers effective CAC.

levers.action: Fix the weakest funnel stage first

name: Bounce Rate
definition: Share of sessions that leave without interacting. Bounced sessions never reach a product, capping the funnel.

parents.note: Bounced sessions never view a product.

levers.action: Match landing pages to ad intent

name: Organic Sessions
definition: Sessions from unpaid search and content.
formula: Sessions attributed to organic search / content

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

name: Paid Sessions
definition: Sessions from paid media (search, social, display).

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

name: Email / CRM Sessions
definition: Sessions from owned email and CRM flows. The lowest-cost, highest-converting channel.
formula: List Size x Send Frequency x Click-Through Rate

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

name: Social Sessions
definition: Sessions from organic and paid social.

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

name: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
definition: Average marketing and sales spend to win one new customer. A cost line; lowering it is money saved.
formula: Marketing & Sales Spend / New Customers Acquired

parents.note: Acquisition spend is an operating cost.

name: Return on Ad Spend (ROAS)
definition: Revenue generated per unit of ad spend. Higher ROAS means cheaper, more efficient acquisition.

parents.note: Higher ROAS lowers effective CAC.

levers.action: Cut wasted spend on poor keywords/audiences

name: Product-View Rate
definition: Share of sessions that view at least one product. The first funnel stage after landing.

parents.note: First multiplicative stage of the funnel.

name: Add-to-Cart Rate
definition: Share of sessions that add a product to the cart. Signals product-page persuasiveness and intent.

parents.note: Second multiplicative stage of the funnel.

name: Internal Search Conversion Rate
definition: Conversion rate of sessions that use on-site search. Searchers signal strong intent and convert well above browsers.
formula: Orders from Search Sessions / Search Sessions x 100

parents.note: Search users convert higher, lifting overall conversion.

name: Search Zero-Result Rate
definition: Share of internal searches that return no results. Each zero-result is a dead end for a high-intent shopper.

parents.note: Zero results kill search conversion.

name: Average Product Rating
definition: Mean star rating across reviewed products. Social proof that lifts conversion and calibrates expectations.
formula: Sum of Star Ratings / Number of Ratings

parents.note: Higher ratings build confidence to add to cart.
Honest, high ratings calibrate expectations and reduce returns.

levers.action: Fix low-rated products at the root

name: Review Coverage Rate
definition: Share of products that have at least one review. Coverage drives the social proof shoppers look for.
formula: Products with >=1 Review / Total Products x 100

parents.note: More reviewed products means more social proof at the point of decision.

levers.action: Incentivize first reviews on new SKUs

name: Images per Product
definition: Average number of images (and rich media) per product page. More angles set expectations and lift intent.
formula: Total Product Images / Number of Products

parents.note: Richer imagery lifts add-to-cart.
Accurate, rich imagery sets expectations and reduces returns.

levers.action: Standardize a minimum image set per SKU

name: Out-of-Stock Rate
definition: Share of catalogue that is unavailable to buy. Out-of-stock items cannot convert.

parents.note: Out-of-stock items cannot be added to cart.

name: Product Page Bounce Rate
definition: Share of product-page sessions that exit without any further action. Signals weak pages or mismatched traffic.
formula: PDP Sessions Exiting Without Action / PDP Sessions x 100

parents.note: High PDP bounce suppresses add-to-cart.

name: Average Order Value (AOV)
definition: Average revenue per order. The basket-size half of revenue and a factor of lifetime value.
formula: Revenue / Orders  (= Items per Order x Average Item Price)

parents.note: The basket-size factor of revenue.
One factor of lifetime value.
Higher AOV lifts contribution per order.

levers.action: Set a free-shipping threshold above AOV

name: Cart-to-Checkout Rate
definition: Share of carts that proceed to start checkout.

parents.note: Third multiplicative stage of the funnel.

levers.action: Show all-in price in the cart

name: Cart Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of created carts abandoned before checkout starts. The classic e-commerce leakage point.

parents.note: Abandonment is the inverse of cart-to-checkout.

levers.action: Remove price surprises in the cart

name: Items per Order
definition: Average number of units in an order. One of the two AOV factors.

parents.note: Units factor of AOV.

name: Average Item Price
definition: Average realized price per unit sold. The price factor of AOV.

parents.note: Price factor of AOV.

name: Cross-Sell Attach Rate
definition: Share of orders that include a cross-sold (complementary) item. A direct lever on items per order.
formula: Orders with >=1 Cross-sold Item / Orders x 100

parents.note: Cross-sell adds units to the basket.

name: Bundle Attach Rate
definition: Share of orders that include a curated multi-product bundle.
formula: Orders with a Bundle / Orders x 100

parents.note: Bundles add units per order.

levers.action: Create value bundles around hero SKUs

name: Upsell Take Rate
definition: Share of orders where the customer accepts a higher-value variant or tier.
formula: Orders with Upsell Accepted / Orders x 100

parents.note: Upselling raises realized item price.

name: Recommendation Click-Through Rate
definition: Share of recommendation impressions that get clicked. Measures how relevant the recommendation engine is.

parents.note: Relevant recommendations drive cross-sell.
Recommendations deepen browsing.

levers.action: Test placement and number of slots

name: Free-Shipping Eligibility Rate
definition: Share of orders that reach the free-shipping threshold. A threshold above AOV nudges bigger baskets, but absorbs shipping on qualifying orders.
formula: Orders Above Free-Shipping Threshold / Orders x 100

parents.note: Chasing the threshold adds items to the basket.
Absorbed shipping on qualifying orders is a cost.

benchmarks.label: AOV uplift
benchmarks.value: ~12-24% higher AOV; ~58% add an item to qualify

name: Discount Rate
definition: Average discount given as a share of gross order value. Discounts cut realized price and come straight off margin.
formula: Discount Value / Gross Order Value x 100

parents.note: Deeper discounts cut realized item price.
Every discount point comes straight off margin.

levers.action: Replace blanket codes with targeted offers
Prefer free shipping over price cuts

name: Checkout Completion Rate
definition: Share of started checkouts that result in a completed order. The final, highest-leverage funnel stage.

parents.note: Final multiplicative stage of the funnel.

benchmarks.label: Checkout usability upside (Baymard)
benchmarks.value: Up to +35% conversion from fixing checkout UX

name: Checkout Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of started checkouts abandoned before completion. The inverse of completion, and a rich source of recoverable revenue.

parents.note: Completion = 100% - abandonment.

levers.action: Remove surprise costs and shorten the form

name: Extra-Cost Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of checkout abandonments caused by surprise shipping, tax or fees. The single biggest abandonment reason.
formula: Abandonments Citing Extra Costs / Abandonments x 100

parents.note: The #1 abandonment reason (48%).

name: Forced-Account Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of checkout abandonments caused by being forced to create an account.
formula: Abandonments Citing Forced Account / Abandonments x 100

parents.note: Forced account creation drives ~26% of abandonment.

name: Security-Distrust Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of checkout abandonments caused by distrust of payment security.
formula: Abandonments Citing Security Concerns / Abandonments x 100

parents.note: Payment-security distrust drives ~25% of abandonment.

levers.action: Show recognizable trust and payment marks

name: Slow-Delivery Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of checkout abandonments caused by delivery being too slow.
formula: Abandonments Citing Slow Delivery / Abandonments x 100

parents.note: Slow delivery drives ~23% of abandonment.

levers.action: Offer and surface faster delivery options

benchmarks.label: Abandonment reason (Baymard)
benchmarks.value: ~23% abandon when delivery is too slow

name: Checkout-Complexity Abandonment Rate
definition: Share of checkout abandonments caused by a long or complicated checkout.
formula: Abandonments Citing Complex Checkout / Abandonments x 100

parents.note: A long/complex checkout drives ~22% of abandonment.

levers.action: Cut steps and fields; add autofill

name: Guest-Checkout Usage Rate
definition: Share of checkouts completed as a guest. High usage means account creation is not a forced barrier.

parents.note: Available guest checkout removes the forced-account barrier.

levers.action: Make guest checkout the default path

name: Payment Methods Offered
definition: Count of locally relevant payment methods available at checkout. Missing a preferred method causes drop-off.
formula: Number of distinct payment methods offered

parents.note: Recognized methods and wallets reduce security-driven abandonment.

name: Checkout Form Fields
definition: Number of fields a shopper must complete to check out. Each extra field adds friction.
formula: Count of required checkout form fields

parents.note: More fields raise complexity-driven abandonment.

name: Average Checkout Duration
definition: Median time to complete checkout once started. Longer checkouts correlate with more drop-off.
formula: Median seconds from checkout start to completion

parents.note: Longer checkouts raise complexity-driven abandonment.

name: Mobile Checkout Completion Rate
definition: Checkout completion rate on mobile, where most traffic is and conversion typically lags desktop.
formula: Mobile Completed Checkouts / Mobile Started Checkouts x 100

parents.note: Mobile is a large component of overall completion.

levers.action: Enable wallets and autofill on mobile
Simplify mobile layout and tap targets

name: Average Checkout Shipping Fee
definition: Average shipping fee shown at checkout. The visible cost most associated with extra-cost abandonment.

parents.note: Higher visible shipping fees raise extra-cost abandonment.

levers.action: Show shipping early, not as a surprise

name: Page Load Time (LCP)
definition: How fast the main content renders, measured as Largest Contentful Paint. Speed influences every funnel stage.

parents.note: Slower pages reduce product views.
Slower pages reduce add-to-cart.
Slow pages raise checkout abandonment.
Slow first paint raises bounce.

name: Interaction to Next Paint (INP)
definition: Responsiveness of the page to user input, a Core Web Vital. Laggy interactions frustrate and suppress action.

parents.note: Laggy interactions suppress add-to-cart.
Unresponsive checkout raises abandonment.

name: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)
definition: Visual stability of the page, a Core Web Vital. Shifting layouts cause mis-taps and erode trust.

parents.note: Layout shift causes mis-taps and lost intent.

levers.action: Reserve space for images and ads

name: Server Response Time (TTFB)
definition: Time for the server to return the first byte. A major, often-overlooked component of total load time.

parents.note: TTFB is a major component of load time.

name: JavaScript Bundle Size
definition: Bytes of JavaScript shipped to the browser. Heavy bundles slow rendering and hurt responsiveness.

parents.note: Large bundles delay rendering.
Heavy JS blocks the main thread, hurting INP.

name: Image Weight per Page
definition: Total image bytes transferred per page. Images are usually the largest part of page weight.

parents.note: Image payload is typically the largest part of page weight.

name: Next-Gen Image Format Adoption
definition: Share of images served in efficient next-gen formats (WebP/AVIF). Higher adoption shrinks image weight.

parents.note: More next-gen formats cut image weight.

name: CDN Cache Hit Rate
definition: Share of requests served from the CDN edge cache rather than origin. Higher hit rates cut response time.
formula: Edge Cache Hits / Total Requests x 100

parents.note: More cache hits mean lower TTFB.

levers.action: Cache HTML at the edge where safe

name: Backend Processing Time
definition: Time the application server spends computing a response before sending it. A direct input to TTFB.
formula: Median server compute time per request (milliseconds)

parents.note: Compute time is a core part of TTFB.

name: Database Query Time
definition: Time spent in database queries per request. Often the dominant share of backend processing time.

parents.note: Query time dominates backend processing.

levers.action: Add indexes and fix N+1 queries

name: Slow Query Rate
definition: Share of database queries exceeding the slow-query threshold. The most-actionable leaf lever behind query time.
formula: Queries Over Threshold / Total Queries x 100

parents.note: Slow queries pull up average query time.

name: Uptime / Availability
definition: Share of time the shop is available and able to take orders. Downtime is lost orders, full stop.

parents.note: Downtime during a session prevents completion.

name: Shipping Cost
definition: Outbound shipping spend per order. A major margin cost line and the most-absorbed cost in e-commerce.

parents.note: Shipping is a variable cost line.
Shipping spend eats contribution margin.

name: Fulfillment Cost per Order
definition: Cost to pick, pack and handle an order in the warehouse. A controllable margin cost.
formula: Pick + Pack + Warehouse + Handling Cost / Orders

parents.note: Fulfillment is a variable cost line.
Fulfillment spend eats contribution margin.

name: Average Delivery Time
definition: Median time from order to doorstep. Slow promised delivery drives abandonment; fast delivery drives loyalty.

parents.note: Slower delivery raises slow-delivery abandonment.
Fast, reliable delivery drives repeat purchases.

name: On-Time Delivery Rate
definition: Share of orders delivered by the promised date. Reliability builds trust and prevents support contacts.

parents.note: Reliable delivery builds loyalty.
On-time delivery removes 'where is my order' contacts.

name: Return Rate
definition: Share of orders returned. A cross-branch KPI: returns reduce net revenue and drive reverse-logistics cost.

parents.note: Returns volume drives reverse-logistics cost.
Returned orders reduce net revenue.

name: Returns Cost
definition: Total cost of handling returns: reverse shipping, inspection, restocking and write-offs.
formula: Reverse Logistics + Restocking + Write-offs per period

parents.note: Returns handling is a variable cost line.
Returns cost eats contribution margin.

levers.action: Lower the return rate at source

name: Reverse Logistics Cost per Return
definition: Cost to process one return: return shipping, inspection and restocking.
formula: Return Shipping + Inspection + Restock Cost / Return

parents.note: Per-return cost scales total returns cost.

name: Carrier Cost per Parcel
definition: Negotiated blended cost per parcel by carrier, zone and weight. Sets the base of shipping cost.

parents.note: Negotiated parcel rates set the base shipping cost.

levers.action: Use regional carriers for some zones

name: Orders per Labor Hour
definition: Warehouse productivity: orders fulfilled per labor hour. Higher productivity lowers per-order fulfillment cost.

parents.note: Higher productivity lowers cost per order.

levers.action: Add automation where volume justifies it

name: Pick Accuracy Rate
definition: Share of orders picked and shipped correctly. Errors cause returns and support contacts.

parents.note: Wrong items get returned.
Wrong items generate support contacts.

name: Repeat Purchase Rate
definition: Share of customers who buy more than once. The core retention KPI; small lifts compound into lifetime value.
formula: Customers with >=2 Orders / Total Customers x 100

parents.note: Returning customers generate additional orders.
More repeat purchases extend lifetime value.

benchmarks.label: Compounding effect
benchmarks.value: 27% buy again after the 1st order; 54% after the 3rd

name: Purchase Frequency
definition: How often a customer orders in a period. A multiplicative factor of lifetime value.

parents.note: Frequency factor of CLV.

name: Customer Lifespan
definition: Average duration a customer keeps buying. A multiplicative factor of lifetime value.

parents.note: Lifespan factor of CLV.

name: Customer Churn Rate
definition: Share of customers who lapse and stop buying in a period. The inverse force on customer lifespan.
formula: Lapsed Customers / Active Customers at Start x 100

parents.note: Higher churn shortens lifespan.

name: Loyalty Enrollment Rate
definition: Share of customers enrolled in the loyalty program. Enrolled members buy more often.

parents.note: Enrolled members repeat more.

levers.action: Reward the second and third purchase

name: Email Click-Through Rate
definition: Share of delivered emails that get clicked. Drives owned-channel sessions and repeat purchases at near-zero marginal cost.

parents.note: Clicks generate email-sourced sessions.
Engaged subscribers come back to buy.

name: Net Promoter Score (NPS)
definition: Willingness of customers to recommend the shop. A leading indicator of retention and word-of-mouth.

parents.note: Happier customers repeat and refer.

levers.action: Close the loop on detractor feedback

name: Subscription Rate
definition: Share of orders placed via subscription. Subscriptions lock in frequency and lifetime value.

parents.note: Subscriptions raise purchase frequency.

name: Total Cost
definition: All money spent to earn revenue: goods, fulfillment, shipping, fees, returns, acquisition and service. Lowering it is money saved.
formula: COGS + Fulfillment + Shipping + Fees + Returns + Acquisition + Cost to Serve

parents.note: Total spend; reducing it is money saved.

levers.action: Attack the largest cost lines first

name: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
definition: Direct landed cost of the products sold. The primary driver of gross margin.
formula: Units Sold x Landed Unit Cost

parents.note: The largest variable cost line for most shops.
COGS is the primary driver of gross margin.

name: Landed Unit Cost
definition: Fully loaded cost to get one unit into the warehouse, ready to sell.
formula: Supplier Price + Inbound Freight + Duties per unit

parents.note: Per-unit landed cost drives total COGS.

name: Inventory Carrying Cost
definition: Annual cost of holding inventory (capital, storage, obsolescence, insurance) as a share of inventory value.
formula: Annual Holding Cost / Average Inventory Value x 100

parents.note: Holding stock ties up cash and incurs cost.
Obsolescence and markdowns erode margin.

name: Payment Processing Fees
definition: Fees paid on each transaction to processors and networks. A small percentage that compounds across every order.

parents.note: A per-transaction cost line.
Fees eat contribution margin on every order.

name: Chargeback Rate
definition: Share of transactions disputed and charged back. Each carries fees and lost goods on top of the refund.

parents.note: Chargebacks add fees and penalties to processing cost.

name: Cost to Serve
definition: Cost of customer support and service per order. Often driven by avoidable delivery and returns issues.

parents.note: Service and support is an operating cost line.

name: Support Contact Rate
definition: Support contacts per 100 orders. Each contact carries a cost; many are avoidable.

parents.note: Each contact adds to cost to serve.

levers.action: Remove 'where is my order' contacts with proactive tracking

name: Self-Service Resolution Rate
definition: Share of customer issues resolved without an agent. Deflects contacts and lowers cost to serve.
formula: Issues Resolved via Self-service / Total Issues x 100

parents.note: More self-service lowers cost to serve.
Self-service deflects agent contacts.

levers.action: Build a help center and order self-service
